reasoning:
The market asks whether Elon Musk and DOGE cut more than $250 billion in federal spending in 2025, resolved via the FRED series "FGEXPND" (Federal Government: Current Expenditures) by comparing the Q4 2024 value to the Q4 2025 value. First, the base rate for this is incredibly low—federal spending almost never decreases nominally year-over-year. The Polymarket price as of October 2025 was 1%. 

I found a search result from a December 2025 article explicitly stating that federal spending actually *increased* under DOGE and Trump. Specifically, the federal government spent $7.6 trillion in the first 11 months of 2025, which was roughly $248 billion *higher* compared to the same 11-month period in 2024, according to a report cited by The Independent. A separate article from December 30, 2025 ("Report: Federal Workforce Down Nine Percent in 2025, but Spending Continued to Grow" in The New American) confirms that despite a 9% cut in the federal workforce, "spending continued to grow." Finally, a NYT investigation into DOGE's claims of saving $200+ billion revealed they were an "accounting mirage" and the identified contracts were still active. Furthermore, DOGE no longer existed as a centralized entity by late 2025 after Elon Musk's exit. Given that overall federal spending heavily increased from Jan–Nov 2025 relative to 2024, there is simply no mathematical way Q4 2025 spending will show a $250 billion nominal drop from Q4 2024 when the Q4 data is finally released. The evidence against this occurring is definitive, and zero evidence points to an actual reduction in spending. I predict this resolves NO.
